name: ETH Deep Value Reclaim v1
thesis: On ETH 4h in chop-to-transition conditions, deeper pullbacks that reclaim value can create denser continuation entries than sparse breakout logic. The edge should come from broad value repair into resumed structure, not exact-touch hero timing.
edge_mechanism: deep pullback reclaim inside structural state
invalidation_condition: Abort if walk-forward results show zero trades on valid data, contradictory filter behavior, weak blind-window participation, or no post-cost edge.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Pullback continuation after a broad reset into value during chop-to-transition conditions.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: When ETH mean reverts back into accepted value without fully breaking structure, continuation can resume with better risk/reward than first-break entries.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is liquid enough for repeated resets and follow-through while still giving enough bars for denser participation.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If market remains one-sided trend without value resets, or if reclaim rules are still too strict, trade count will collapse.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If trade count is low but nonzero, widen reclaim distance or relax directional confirmation before changing exits.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; direction filter and reclaim trigger point the same way
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: reduced versus prior zero-trade grammar by using broader reclaim thresholds
